name: Tyap
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Tyap is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Tyap (a̱lyem Tyap) is a Plateau (Atlantic-Congo) language of Kaduna State, Nigeria, written in the Latin script (with the macron-below vowel ⟨a̱⟩ and other digraphs) and tonal; an indigenous Akai script also exists but is marginal. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script Tyap orthography.

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
